text: Two standard thoracoscopic ports are visible in the right hemithorax. The surgeon inspects the area to identify the redundant diaphragm and places a linear clamp across it.

The surgeon carefully adjusts the tension to sculpt the contour for the staple line during the diaphragmatic placation. A linear stapler is positioned beneath the cross clamp, and the staple line is advanced medially. The cross clamp placement is revised after each staple load to ensure proper tension and contour.
The surgeon completes the staple line medially, removes the specimen, and inspects the peritoneal surface of the diaphragm. The staple line is then reinforced by over-sewing.
The surgeon inspects the diaphragmatic placation, visually assessing the tension and contour of the staple line to ensure proper placement and effectiveness.
